message.content: fait un inventaire des skilss et aggents installés

content.thinking: The user is asking me to make an inventory of installed skills and agents. They mentioned in their CLAUDE.md that I should consult `C:\Users\User\.claude\claude-capabilities.md` at the start of each session. Let me read that file and also compile the information from the system reminder about available skills.

content.text: Let me consulter le fichier de référence des capabilities.

   222→| `/gsd:complete-milestone` | Archiver un milestone termine et preparer le suivant |
   223→

   225→
   226→| Commande | Description |
   227→|----------|-------------|

   230→| `/gsd:quick` | Executer une tache rapide avec les garanties GSD |

   233→
   234→| Commande | Description |
   235→|----------|-------------|
   236→| `/gsd:add-todo` | Capturer une idee ou tache comme todo |
   237→| `/gsd:check-todos` | Lister les todos en attente et en selectionner un |
   238→| `/gsd:cleanup` | Archiver les repertoires de phases completees |
   239→| `/gsd:debug` | Debugging systematique avec etat persistant |

   242→
   243→| Commande | Description |
   244→|----------|-------------|
   245→| `/gsd:settings` | Configurer les toggles et profil de modele |
   246→| `/gsd:set-profile` | Changer le profil de modele (quality/balanced/budget) |
   247→| `/gsd:update` | Mettre a jour GSD avec affichage du changelog |
   248→| `/gsd:reapply-patches` | Reappliquer les modifications locales apres une MAJ |
   249→| `/gsd:help` | Afficher les commandes GSD et guide d'utilisation |
   250→| `/gsd:join-discord` | Rejoindre la communaute Discord GSD |
   251→
   252→---
   253→
   254→## Agents (sous-agents specialises)
   255→
   256→Les agents sont des processus autonomes lances pour gerer des taches complexes.
   257→
   258→### Agents generaux
   259→
   260→| Agent | Description |
   261→|-------|-------------|
   262→| **Bash** | Specialiste d'execution de commandes bash, operations git, taches terminal |
   263→| **general-purpose** | Agent generaliste pour recherches complexes, recherche de code, taches multi-etapes |
   264→| **Explore** | Agent rapide pour explorer les codebases : recherche de fichiers, mots-cles, comprehension du code |
   265→| **Plan** | Architecte logiciel pour designer des plans d'implementation, identifier les fichiers critiques |
   266→| **code-reviewer** | Review de code apres completion d'une etape majeure du projet |
   267→
   268→### Agents GSD
   269→
   270→| Agent | Description |
   271→|-------|-------------|
   272→| **gsd-executor** | Execute les plans GSD avec commits atomiques et gestion d'etat |
   273→| **gsd-planner** | Cree des plans de phase executables avec analyse de dependances |
   274→| **gsd-debugger** | Investigation de bugs avec methode scientifique et checkpoints |
   275→| **gsd-verifier** | Verifie l'atteinte des objectifs de phase par analyse goal-backward |
   276→| **gsd-roadmapper** | Cree des roadmaps projet avec decomposition en phases |
   277→| **gsd-project-researcher** | Recherche l'ecosysteme du domaine avant creation de roadmap |
   278→| **gsd-phase-researcher** | Recherche comment implementer une phase avant planification |
   279→| **gsd-research-synthesizer** | Synthetise les outputs de recherche en SUMMARY.md |
   280→| **gsd-plan-checker** | Verifie que les plans atteindront l'objectif de phase |
   281→| **gsd-integration-checker** | Verifie l'integration cross-phases et les flux E2E |
   282→| **gsd-codebase-mapper** | Explore le codebase et ecrit des documents d'analyse structures |
   283→
   284→### Agents utilitaires
   285→
   286→| Agent | Description |
   287→|-------|-------------|
   288→| **statusline-setup** | Configure la status line de Claude Code |
   289→| **claude-code-guide** | Repond aux questions sur Claude Code, Agent SDK, API Claude |
   290→
   291→---
   292→
   293→## Superpowers
   294→
   295→Le skill `using-superpowers` etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills de maniere optimale. Il s'active au demarrage de toute conversation pour :
   296→
   297→- **Decouverte de skills** : Trouver automatiquement le skill adapte a la tache
   298→- **Chargement a la demande** : Invoquer les skills via `/nom-du-skill`
   299→- **Composition** : Combiner plusieurs skills pour des workflows complexes
   300→- **Parallelisation** : Lancer plusieurs agents en parallele pour des taches independantes
   301→
   302→### Capacites cles
   303→
   304→- **Recherche web** : `WebSearch` et `WebFetch` pour acceder a des informations a jour
   305→- **Systeme de fichiers** : Lecture, ecriture, edition, recherche de fichiers
   306→- **Execution de commandes** : Terminal bash pour git, npm, docker, etc.
   307→- **Memoire persistante** : Knowledge graph pour stocker et recuperer des informations
   308→- **Pensee sequentielle** : Analyse structuree de problemes complexes
   309→- **Mode plan** : Planification avant implementation avec approbation utilisateur
   310→- **Gestion de taches** : Suivi de progression avec creation/mise a jour de taches
   311→- **Worktrees** : Isolation du travail dans des branches separees
   312→
   313→---
   314→
   315→*Fichier genere le 2026-02-23 par Claude Code (Opus 4.6)*
   316→

| `mcp-builder` | Guide pour creer des serveurs MCP de qualite |
| `skill-creator` | Guide pour creer des skills efficaces |
| `agent-skill-creator` | Creation d'agents et automatisation de taches repetitives |
| `skill-reviewer` | Review et amelioration de skills Claude Code |
| `skills-search` | Recherche, decouverte, installation de skills |
| `writing-skills` | Creation, edition, verification de skills |
| `configure-ecc` | Installateur interactif Everything Claude Code |

### Internationalisation & Communication

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `i18n-expert` | Internationalisation et localisation |
| `internal-comms` | Communications internes avec formats adaptes |
| `scrum-master` | Scrum Master avance : analyse sante equipe, velocity, retros |
| `senior-pm` | Chef de projet senior : logiciel enterprise, SaaS, transformation digitale |

### Patterns avances

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `content-hash-cache-pattern` | Cache par hash SHA-256 du contenu des fichiers |
| `cost-aware-llm-pipeline` | Optimisation de couts pour usage API LLM |
| `regex-vs-llm-structured-text` | Framework de decision regex vs LLM pour parsing de texte |
| `continuous-learning` | Extraction de patterns reutilisables depuis les sessions |
| `continuous-learning-v2` | Systeme d'apprentissage par instincts via hooks |

### Utilitaires rapides

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `lint` | Assistant Linting |
| `debug` | Assistant Debug |
| `refactor` | Assistant Refactoring |
| `npm-scripts` | Assistant NPM Scripts |
| `ultra-think` | Analyse profonde et resolution de problemes multi-dimensionnelle |
| `keybindings-help` | Personnalisation des raccourcis clavier |
| `claude-developer-platform` | Programmes utilisant l'API Claude / Anthropic |
| `using-superpowers` | Etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills |
| `dispatching-parallel-agents` | Parallelisation de taches independantes |
| `subagent-driven-development` | Execution de plans avec taches independantes en session |
| `systematic-debugging` | Debugging systematique avant de proposer des corrections |

---

## GSD (Get Stuff Done) - Commandes

Systeme de gestion de projet structure avec roadmap, phases, milestones et execution.

### Gestion de projet

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:new-project` | Initialiser un nouveau projet avec context gathering et PROJECT.md |
| `/gsd:new-milestone` | Demarrer un nouveau cycle de milestone |
| `/gsd:progress` | Verifier la progression du projet et router vers l'action suivante |
| `/gsd:map-codebase` | Analyser le codebase avec des agents mappers paralleles |
| `/gsd:health` | Diagnostiquer la sante du repertoire de planning |

### Planification

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:plan-phase` | Creer un plan de phase detaille (PLAN.md) avec verification |
| `/gsd:discuss-phase` | Questions adaptatives avant la planification d'une phase |
| `/gsd:list-phase-assumptions` | Reveler les hypotheses de Claude sur l'approche d'une phase |
| `/gsd:research-phase` | Rechercher comment implementer une phase |
| `/gsd:plan-milestone-gaps` | Creer des phases pour combler les lacunes d'un audit milestone |

### Gestion des phases

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:add-phase` | Ajouter une phase a la fin du milestone courant |
| `/gsd:insert-phase` | Inserer du travail urgent comme phase decimale (ex: 72.1) |
| `/gsd:remove-phase` | Supprimer une phase future et renumeroter |
| `/gsd:execute-phase` | Executer tous les plans d'une phase avec parallelisation par vagues |

### Verification & Completion

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:verify-work` | Valider les features construites via UAT conversationnel |
| `/gsd:audit-milestone` | Auditer la completion d'un milestone avant archivage |
| `/gsd:complete-milestone` | Archiver un milestone termine et preparer le suivant |

### Gestion de session

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:resume-work` | Reprendre le travail d'une session precedente |
| `/gsd:pause-work` | Creer un handoff de contexte lors d'une pause |
| `/gsd:quick` | Executer une tache rapide avec les garanties GSD |

### Taches & Maintenance

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:add-todo` | Capturer une idee ou tache comme todo |
| `/gsd:check-todos` | Lister les todos en attente et en selectionner un |
| `/gsd:cleanup` | Archiver les repertoires de phases completees |
| `/gsd:debug` | Debugging systematique avec etat persistant |

### Configuration & Aide

| Commande | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `/gsd:settings` | Configurer les toggles et profil de modele |
| `/gsd:set-profile` | Changer le profil de modele (quality/balanced/budget) |
| `/gsd:update` | Mettre a jour GSD avec affichage du changelog |
| `/gsd:reapply-patches` | Reappliquer les modifications locales apres une MAJ |
| `/gsd:help` | Afficher les commandes GSD et guide d'utilisation |
| `/gsd:join-discord` | Rejoindre la communaute Discord GSD |

---

## Agents (sous-agents specialises)

Les agents sont des processus autonomes lances pour gerer des taches complexes.

### Agents generaux

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **Bash** | Specialiste d'execution de commandes bash, operations git, taches terminal |
| **general-purpose** | Agent generaliste pour recherches complexes, recherche de code, taches multi-etapes |
| **Explore** | Agent rapide pour explorer les codebases : recherche de fichiers, mots-cles, comprehension du code |
| **Plan** | Architecte logiciel pour designer des plans d'implementation, identifier les fichiers critiques |
| **code-reviewer** | Review de code apres completion d'une etape majeure du projet |

### Agents GSD

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **gsd-executor** | Execute les plans GSD avec commits atomiques et gestion d'etat |
| **gsd-planner** | Cree des plans de phase executables avec analyse de dependances |
| **gsd-debugger** | Investigation de bugs avec methode scientifique et checkpoints |
| **gsd-verifier** | Verifie l'atteinte des objectifs de phase par analyse goal-backward |
| **gsd-roadmapper** | Cree des roadmaps projet avec decomposition en phases |
| **gsd-project-researcher** | Recherche l'ecosysteme du domaine avant creation de roadmap |
| **gsd-phase-researcher** | Recherche comment implementer une phase avant planification |
| **gsd-research-synthesizer** | Synthetise les outputs de recherche en SUMMARY.md |
| **gsd-plan-checker** | Verifie que les plans atteindront l'objectif de phase |
| **gsd-integration-checker** | Verifie l'integration cross-phases et les flux E2E |
| **gsd-codebase-mapper** | Explore le codebase et ecrit des documents d'analyse structures |

### Agents utilitaires

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| **statusline-setup** | Configure la status line de Claude Code |
| **claude-code-guide** | Repond aux questions sur Claude Code, Agent SDK, API Claude |

---

## Superpowers

Le skill `using-superpowers` etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills de maniere optimale. Il s'active au demarrage de toute conversation pour :

- **Decouverte de skills** : Trouver automatiquement le skill adapte a la tache
- **Chargement a la demande** : Invoquer les skills via `/nom-du-skill`
- **Composition** : Combiner plusieurs skills pour des workflows complexes
- **Parallelisation** : Lancer plusieurs agents en parallele pour des taches independantes

### Capacites cles

- **Recherche web** : `WebSearch` et `WebFetch` pour acceder a des informations a jour
- **Systeme de fichiers** : Lecture, ecriture, edition, recherche de fichiers
- **Execution de commandes** : Terminal bash pour git, npm, docker, etc.
- **Memoire persistante** : Knowledge graph pour stocker et recuperer des informations
- **Pensee sequentielle** : Analyse structuree de problemes complexes
- **Mode plan** : Planification avant implementation avec approbation utilisateur
- **Gestion de taches** : Suivi de progression avec creation/mise a jour de taches
- **Worktrees** : Isolation du travail dans des branches separees

---

*Fichier genere le 2026-02-23 par Claude Code (Opus 4.6)*

input.prompt:
I need a complete inventory of all installed skills and plugins in the Claude Code environment. 

Search these locations thoroughly:
1. C:\Users\User\.claude\plugins\ - list all plugins and their skills
2. C:\Users\User\.claude\skills\ - any standalone skills
3. C:\Users\User\.claude\commands\ - any custom commands

For each plugin found in C:\Users\User\.claude\plugins\marketplaces\claude-plugins-official\plugins\, list the plugin name and count the skills it contains.

Also search for anything named "whobson" anywhere under C:\Users\User\.claude\ and C:\Users\User\

Give me:
- Total count of all unique skills found
- Total count of all plugins/agents
- Any reference to "whobson" or "112 agents"
- The complete list organized by category

content.content:
1→# Claude Code - Skills, GSD, Superpowers & Agents
     2→
     3→## Skills disponibles
     4→
     5→### Developpement & Architecture
     6→
     7→| Skill | Description |
     8→|-------|-------------|
     9→| `api-design` | REST API design patterns : naming, status codes, pagination, filtering, error handling |
    10→| `backend-patterns` | Architecture backend, API design, optimisation BDD, best practices serveur |
    11→| `frontend-patterns` | Patterns frontend pour React, Next.js, state management, performance |
    12→| `frontend-design` | Interfaces frontend production-grade avec haute qualite design |
    13→| `senior-architect` | Design d'architecture systeme, evaluation microservices |
    14→| `senior-backend` | Design REST APIs, optimisation BDD, queries |
    15→| `senior-frontend` | Developpement frontend moderne et performant |
    16→| `senior-fullstack` | Fullstack : scaffolding Next.js/FastAPI/MERN/Django |
    17→| `senior-data-engineer` | Pipelines de donnees scalables, ETL/ELT |
    18→| `senior-devops` | CI/CD, infrastructure automation, containerisation |
    19→| `project-guidelines-example` | Template de guidelines projet base sur une app de production |
    20→| `tech-stack-evaluator` | Evaluation de stack technologique avec analyse TCO et securite |
    21→
    22→### Frameworks & Langages
    23→
    24→| Skill | Description |
    25→|-------|-------------|
    26→| `python-patterns` | Idiomes Python, PEP 8, type hints, best practices |
    27→| `python-testing` | Strategies de test Python avec pytest, TDD, fixtures, mocking |
    28→| `golang-patterns` | Patterns Go idiomatiques, best practices |
    29→| `golang-testing` | Tests Go : table-driven, subtests, benchmarks, fuzzing |
    30→| `java-coding-standards` | Standards Java pour Spring Boot services |
    31→| `cpp-coding-standards` | Standards C++ bases sur les C++ Core Guidelines |
    32→| `cpp-testing` | Tests C++ avec GoogleTest/CTest |
    33→| `swift-actor-persistence` | Persistence thread-safe en Swift avec actors |
    34→| `swift-protocol-di-testing` | Injection de dependances par protocoles en Swift |
    35→| `typescript-migrate` | Assistant de migration TypeScript |
    36→| `django-patterns` | Architecture Django, REST API avec DRF, ORM |
    37→| `django-security` | Securite Django : auth, CSRF, SQL injection |
    38→| `django-tdd` | TDD Django avec pytest-django, factory_boy |
    39→| `django-verification` | Boucle de verification Django : migrations, linting, tests |
    40→| `springboot-patterns` | Architecture Spring Boot, REST API, services |
    41→| `springboot-security` | Spring Security best practices |
    42→| `springboot-tdd` | TDD Spring Boot avec JUnit 5, Mockito |
    43→| `springboot-verification` | Boucle de verification Spring Boot |
    44→| `jpa-patterns` | JPA/Hibernate : entites, relations, queries, transactions |
    45→| `clickhouse-io` | ClickHouse : patterns BDD, optimisation queries, analytics |
    46→| `postgres-patterns` | PostgreSQL : optimisation queries, schema, indexing, securite |
    47→
    48→### Testing & Qualite
    49→
    50→| Skill | Description |
    51→|-------|-------------|
    52→| `test` | Assistant de tests |
    53→| `test-driven-development` | TDD : ecrire les tests avant l'implementation |
    54→| `tdd-workflow` | Workflow TDD pour features, bugfix, refactoring |
    55→| `e2e-testing` | Tests E2E avec Playwright : Page Object Model, CI/CD |
    56→| `webapp-testing` | Tests d'applications web locales avec Playwright |
    57→| `qa-expert` | Processus de QA testing complet |
    58→| `senior-qa` | Generation de tests, tests unitaires, analyse |
    59→| `coding-standards` | Standards de code universels : TypeScript, JavaScript, React, Python, Go |
    60→| `code-reviewer` | Revue de code automatisee pour TS, JS, Python, Go, Swift, Kotlin |
    61→| `verification-loop` | Systeme de verification complet pour sessions Claude Code |
    62→| `verification-before-completion` | Verification avant de declarer un travail termine |
    63→| `eval-harness` | Framework d'evaluation formel pour sessions Claude Code |
    64→
    65→### Securite
    66→
    67→| Skill | Description |

    69→| `security-review` | Review securite pour auth, user input, secrets, creation |

    71→| `senior-security` | Threat modeling, analyse vulnerabilites, architecture securisee |

    73→
    74→### DevOps & Deployment

    79→| `deployment-patterns` | Workflows de deploiement, CI/CD, Docker, health checks, rollback |
    80→| `database-migrations` | Migrations BDD : schema, data, rollbacks, zero-downtime |
    81→| `aws-solution-architect` | Architectures AWS serverless avec IaC templates |
    82→
    83→### Documents & Media
    84→
    85→| Skill | Description |
    86→|-------|-------------|
    87→| `pdf` | Lecture, creation, manipulation de fichiers PDF |
    88→| `pdf-creator` | Creation de PDF depuis markdown avec support fonts chinoises |
    89→| `docx` | Creation, lecture, edition de documents Word |
    90→| `pptx` | Manipulation de fichiers PowerPoint |
    91→| `ppt-creator` | Creation de slide decks professionnels |
    92→| `xlsx` | Manipulation de fichiers tableurs |
    93→| `markdown-tools` | Conversion de documents en markdown multi-outils |
    94→| `mermaid-tools` | Extraction de diagrammes Mermaid et generation PNG |
    95→
    96→### Design & UI
    97→
    98→| Skill | Description |
    99→|-------|-------------|
   100→| `algorithmic-art` | Art algorithmique avec p5.js, randomness seedee, parametres interactifs |
   101→| `canvas-design` | Art visuel en .png et .pdf avec philosophie design |
   102→| `brand-guidelines` | Couleurs et typographie officielles Anthropic |
   103→| `theme-factory` | Toolkit de theming pour slides, docs, reportings |
   104→| `ui-ux-pro-max` | Design UI/UX : 67 styles, 96 palettes, 57 font pairings, 25 charts |
   105→| `web-artifacts-builder` | Creation d'artifacts HTML multi-composants |
   106→| `slack-gif-creator` | Creation de GIFs animes optimises pour Slack |
   107→
   108→### Productivite & Workflow
   109→
   110→| Skill | Description |
   111→|-------|-------------|
   112→| `brainstorming` | A utiliser avant tout travail creatif |
   113→| `deep-research` | Rapports de recherche avec citations et suivi des preuves |
   114→| `fact-checker` | Verification de faits via recherche web et sources officielles |
   115→| `prompt-optimizer` | Transformation de prompts vagues en specifications precises (EARS) |
   116→| `senior-prompt-engineer` | Optimisation de prompts, design de templates |
   117→| `doc-coauthoring` | Co-redaction de documentation structuree |
   118→| `writing-plans` | Planification avant le code quand il y a des specs/requirements |
   119→| `executing-plans` | Execution de plans d'implementation avec review |
   120→| `search-first` | Recherche avant codage : trouver les outils existants d'abord |
   121→| `strategic-compact` | Compaction manuelle du contexte a intervalles logiques |
   122→| `iterative-retrieval` | Raffinement progressif de la recuperation de contexte |
   123→
   124→### Git & GitHub
   125→
   126→| Skill | Description |
   127→|-------|-------------|
   128→| `github-ops` | Operations GitHub completes via gh CLI et API |
   129→| `using-git-worktrees` | Travail isole avec git worktrees |
   130→| `finishing-a-development-branch` | Finalisation d'une branche de dev : merge, integration |
   131→| `requesting-code-review` | Demande de code review apres completion |
   132→| `receiving-code-review` | Reception et implementation de feedback de review |
   133→
   134→### MCP & Skills
   135→
   136→| Skill | Description |
   137→|-------|-------------|
   138→| `mcp-builder` | Guide pour creer des serveurs MCP de qualite |
   139→| `skill-creator` | Guide pour creer des skills efficaces |
   140→| `agent-skill-creator` | Creation d'agents et automatisation de taches repetitives |
   141→| `skill-reviewer` | Review et amelioration de skills Claude Code |
   142→| `skills-search` | Recherche, decouverte, installation de skills |
   143→| `writing-skills` | Creation, edition, verification de skills |
   144→| `configure-ecc` | Installateur interactif Everything Claude Code |
   145→
   146→### Internationalisation & Communication
   147→
   148→| Skill | Description |
   149→|-------|-------------|
   150→| `i18n-expert` | Internationalisation et localisation |
   151→| `internal-comms` | Communications internes avec formats adaptes |
   152→| `scrum-master` | Scrum Master avance : analyse sante equipe, velocity, retros |
   153→| `senior-pm` | Chef de projet senior : logiciel enterprise, SaaS, transformation digitale |
   154→
   155→### Patterns avances
   156→
   157→| Skill | Description |
   158→|-------|-------------|
   159→| `content-hash-cache-pattern` | Cache par hash SHA-256 du contenu des fichiers |
   160→| `cost-aware-llm-pipeline` | Optimisation de couts pour usage API LLM |
   161→| `regex-vs-llm-structured-text` | Framework de decision regex vs LLM pour parsing de texte |
   162→| `continuous-learning` | Extraction de patterns reutilisables depuis les sessions |
   163→| `continuous-learning-v2` | Systeme d'apprentissage par instincts via hooks |
   164→
   165→### Utilitaires rapides
   166→
   167→| Skill | Description |
   168→|-------|-------------|
   169→| `lint` | Assistant Linting |
   170→| `debug` | Assistant Debug |
   171→| `refactor` | Assistant Refactoring |
   172→| `npm-scripts` | Assistant NPM Scripts |
   173→| `ultra-think` | Analyse profonde et resolution de problemes multi-dimensionnelle |
   174→| `keybindings-help` | Personnalisation des raccourcis clavier |
   175→| `claude-developer-platform` | Programmes utilisant l'API Claude / Anthropic |
   176→| `using-superpowers` | Etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills |
   177→| `dispatching-parallel-agents` | Parallelisation de taches independantes |
   178→| `subagent-driven-development` | Execution de plans avec taches independantes en session |
   179→| `systematic-debugging` | Debugging systematique avant de proposer des corrections |
   180→
   181→---
   182→
   183→## GSD (Get Stuff Done) - Commandes
   184→
   185→Systeme de gestion de projet structure avec roadmap, phases, milestones et execution.
   186→
   187→### Gestion de projet
   188→
   189→| Commande | Description |
   190→|----------|-------------|
   191→| `/gsd:new-project` | Initialiser un nouveau projet avec context gathering et PROJECT.md |
   192→| `/gsd:new-milestone` | Demarrer un nouveau cycle de milestone |
   193→| `/gsd:progress` | Verifier la progression du projet et router vers l'action suivante |
   194→| `/gsd:map-codebase` | Analyser le codebase avec des agents mappers paralleles |
   195→| `/gsd:health` | Diagnostiquer la sante du repertoire de planning |
   196→
   197→### Planification
   198→
   199→| Commande | Description |
   200→|----------|-------------|
   201→| `/gsd:plan-phase` | Creer un plan de phase detaille (PLAN.md) avec verification |
   202→| `/gsd:discuss-phase` | Questions adaptatives avant la planification d'une phase |
   203→| `/gsd:list-phase-assumptions` | Reveler les hypotheses de Claude sur l'approche d'une phase |
   204→| `/gsd:research-phase` | Rechercher comment implementer une phase |
   205→| `/gsd:plan-milestone-gaps` | Creer des phases pour combler les lacunes d'un audit milestone |
   206→
   207→### Gestion des phases
   208→
   209→| Commande | Description |
   210→|----------|-------------|
   211→| `/gsd:add-phase` | Ajouter une phase a la fin du milestone courant |
   212→| `/gsd:insert-phase` | Inserer du travail urgent comme phase decimale (ex: 72.1) |
   213→| `/gsd:remove-phase` | Supprimer une phase future et renumeroter |
   214→| `/gsd:execute-phase` | Executer tous les plans d'une phase avec parallelisation par vagues |
   215→
   216→### Verification & Completion
   217→
   218→| Commande | Description |
   219→|----------|-------------|
   220→| `/gsd:verify-work` | Valider les features construites via UAT conversationnel |
   221→| `/gsd:audit-milestone` | Auditer la completion d'un milestone avant archivage |
   222→| `/gsd:complete-milestone` | Archiver un milestone termine et preparer le suivant |
   223→
   224→### Gestion de session
   225→
   226→| Commande | Description |
   227→|----------|-------------|
   228→| `/gsd:resume-work` | Reprendre le travail d'une session precedente |
   229→| `/gsd:pause-work` | Creer un handoff de contexte lors d'une pause |
   230→| `/gsd:quick` | Executer une tache rapide avec les garanties GSD |
   231→
   232→### Taches & Maintenance
   233→
   234→| Commande | Description |
   235→|----------|-------------|
   236→| `/gsd:add-todo` | Capturer une idee ou tache comme todo |
   237→| `/gsd:check-todos` | Lister les todos en attente et en selectionner un |
   238→| `/gsd:cleanup` | Archiver les repertoires de phases completees |
   239→| `/gsd:debug` | Debugging systematique avec etat persistant |
   240→
   241→### Configuration & Aide
   242→
   243→| Commande | Description |
   244→|----------|-------------|
   245→| `/gsd:settings` | Configurer les toggles et profil de modele |
   246→| `/gsd:set-profile` | Changer le profil de modele (quality/balanced/budget) |
   247→| `/gsd:update` | Mettre a jour GSD avec affichage du changelog |
   248→| `/gsd:reapply-patches` | Reappliquer les modifications locales apres une MAJ |
   249→| `/gsd:help` | Afficher les commandes GSD et guide d'utilisation |
   250→| `/gsd:join-discord` | Rejoindre la communaute Discord GSD |
   251→
   252→---
   253→
   254→## Agents (sous-agents specialises)
   255→
   256→Les agents sont des processus autonomes lances pour gerer des taches complexes.
   257→
   258→### Agents generaux
   259→
   260→| Agent | Description |
   261→|-------|-------------|
   262→| **Bash** | Specialiste d'execution de commandes bash, operations git, taches terminal |
   263→| **general-purpose** | Agent generaliste pour recherches complexes, recherche de code, taches multi-etapes |
   264→| **Explore** | Agent rapide pour explorer les codebases : recherche de fichiers, mots-cles, comprehension du code |
   265→| **Plan** | Architecte logiciel pour designer des plans d'implementation, identifier les fichiers critiques |
   266→| **code-reviewer** | Review de code apres completion d'une etape majeure du projet |
   267→
   268→### Agents GSD
   269→
   270→| Agent | Description |
   271→|-------|-------------|
   272→| **gsd-executor** | Execute les plans GSD avec commits atomiques et gestion d'etat |
   273→| **gsd-planner** | Cree des plans de phase executables avec analyse de dependances |
   274→| **gsd-debugger** | Investigation de bugs avec methode scientifique et checkpoints |
   275→| **gsd-verifier** | Verifie l'atteinte des objectifs de phase par analyse goal-backward |
   276→| **gsd-roadmapper** | Cree des roadmaps projet avec decomposition en phases |
   277→| **gsd-project-researcher** | Recherche l'ecosysteme du domaine avant creation de roadmap |
   278→| **gsd-phase-researcher** | Recherche comment implementer une phase avant planification |
   279→| **gsd-research-synthesizer** | Synthetise les outputs de recherche en SUMMARY.md |
   280→| **gsd-plan-checker** | Verifie que les plans atteindront l'objectif de phase |
   281→| **gsd-integration-checker** | Verifie l'integration cross-phases et les flux E2E |
   282→| **gsd-codebase-mapper** | Explore le codebase et ecrit des documents d'analyse structures |
   283→
   284→### Agents utilitaires
   285→
   286→| Agent | Description |
   287→|-------|-------------|
   288→| **statusline-setup** | Configure la status line de Claude Code |
   289→| **claude-code-guide** | Repond aux questions sur Claude Code, Agent SDK, API Claude |
   290→
   291→---
   292→
   293→## Superpowers
   294→
   295→Le skill `using-superpowers` etablit comment trouver et utiliser les skills de maniere optimale. Il s'active au demarrage de toute conversation pour :
   296→
   297→- **Decouverte de skills** : Trouver automatiquement le skill adapte a la tache
   298→- **Chargement a la demande** : Invoquer les skills via `/nom-du-skill`
   299→- **Composition** : Combiner plusieurs skills pour des workflows complexes
   300→- **Parallelisation** : Lancer plusieurs agents en parallele pour des taches independantes
   301→
   302→### Capacites cles
   303→
   304→- **Recherche web** : `WebSearch` et `WebFetch` pour acceder a des informations a jour
   305→- **Systeme de fichiers** : Lecture, ecriture, edition, recherche de fichiers
   306→- **Execution de commandes** : Terminal bash pour git, npm, docker, etc.
   307→- **Memoire persistante** : Knowledge graph pour stocker et recuperer des informations
   308→- **Pensee sequentielle** : Analyse structuree de problemes complexes
   309→- **Mode plan** : Planification avant implementation avec approbation utilisateur
   310→- **Gestion de taches** : Suivi de progression avec creation/mise a jour de taches
   311→- **Worktrees** : Isolation du travail dans des branches separees
   312→
   313→---
   314→
   315→*Fichier genere le 2026-02-23 par Claude Code (Opus 4.6)*
   316→

- The complete list organized by category

content.content:
1→# Claude Code Plugins: Orchestration and Automation
     2→
     3→> **⚡ Updated for Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6 & Haiku 4.5** — Three-tier model strategy for optimal performance
     4→
     5→[![Run in Smithery](https://smithery.ai/badge/skills/wshobson)](https://smithery.ai/skills?ns=wshobson&utm_source=github&utm_medium=badge)
     6→
     7→> **🎯 Agent Skills Enabled** — 146 specialized skills extend Claude's capabilities across plugins with progressive disclosure
     8→
     9→A comprehensive production-ready system combining **112 specialized AI agents**, **16 multi-agent workflow orchestrators**, **146 agent skills**, and **79 development tools** organized into **72 focused, single-purpose plugins** for [Claude Code](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview).
    10→
    11→## Overview
    12→
    13→This unified repository provides everything needed for intelligent automation and multi-agent orchestration across modern software development:
    14→
    15→- **72 Focused Plugins** - Granular, single-purpose plugins optimized for minimal token usage and composability
    16→- **112 Specialized Agents** - Domain experts with deep knowledge across architecture, languages, infrastructure, quality, data/AI, documentation, business operations, and SEO
    17→- **146 Agent Skills** - Modular knowledge packages with progressive disclosure for specialized expertise
    18→- **16 Workflow Orchestrators** - Multi-agent coordination systems for complex operations like full-stack development, security hardening, ML pipelines, and incident response
    19→- **79 Development Tools** - Optimized utilities including project scaffolding, security scanning, test automation, and infrastructure setup
    20→
    21→### Key Features
    22→
    23→- **Granular Plugin Architecture**: 72 focused plugins optimized for minimal token usage
    24→- **Comprehensive Tooling**: 79 development tools including test generation, scaffolding, and security scanning
    25→- **100% Agent Coverage**: All plugins include specialized agents
    26→- **Agent Skills**: 146 specialized skills following for progressive disclosure and token efficiency
    27→- **Clear Organization**: 23 categories with 1-6 plugins each for easy discovery
    28→- **Efficient Design**: Average 3.4 components per plugin (follows Anthropic's 2-8 pattern)
    29→
    30→### How It Works
    31→
    32→Each plugin is completely isolated with its own agents, commands, and skills:
    33→
    34→- **Install only what you need** - Each plugin loads only its specific agents, commands, and skills
    35→- **Minimal token usage** - No unnecessary resources loaded into context
    36→- **Mix and match** - Compose multiple plugins for complex workflows
    37→- **Clear boundaries** - Each plugin has a single, focused purpose
    38→- **Progressive disclosure** - Skills load knowledge only when activated
    39→
    40→**Example**: Installing `python-development` loads 3 Python agents, 1 scaffolding tool, and makes 16 skills available (~1000 tokens), not the entire marketplace.
    41→
    42→## Quick Start
    43→
    44→### Step 1: Add the Marketplace
    45→
    46→Add this marketplace to Claude Code:
    47→
    48→```bash
    49→/plugin marketplace add wshobson/agents
    50→```
    51→
    52→This makes all 72 plugins available for installation, but **does not load any agents or tools** into your context.
    53→
    54→### Step 2: Install Plugins
    55→
    56→Browse available plugins:
    57→
    58→```bash
    59→/plugin
    60→```
    61→
    62→Install the plugins you need:
    63→
    64→```bash
    65→# Essential development plugins
    66→/plugin install python-development          # Python with 16 specialized skills
    67→/plugin install javascript-typescript       # JS/TS with 4 specialized skills
    68→/plugin install backend-development         # Backend APIs with 3 architecture skills
    69→
    70→# Infrastructure & operations
    71→/plugin install kubernetes-operations       # K8s with 4 deployment skills
    72→/plugin install cloud-infrastructure        # AWS/Azure/GCP with 4 cloud skills
    73→
    74→# Security & quality
    75→/plugin install security-scanning           # SAST with security skill
    76→/plugin install comprehensive-review       # Multi-perspective code analysis
    77→
    78→# Full-stack orchestration
    79→/plugin install full-stack-orchestration   # Multi-agent workflows
    80→```
    81→
    82→Each installed plugin loads **only its specific agents, commands, and skills** into Claude's context.
    83→
    84→### Plugins vs Agents
    85→
    86→You install **plugins**, which bundle agents:
    87→
    88→| Plugin                  | Agents                                            |
    89→| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
    90→| `comprehensive-review`  | architect-review, code-reviewer, security-auditor |
    91→| `javascript-typescript` | javascript-pro, typescript-pro                    |
    92→| `python-development`    | python-pro, django-pro, fastapi-pro               |
    93→| `blockchain-web3`       | blockchain-developer                              |
    94→
    95→```bash
    96→# ❌ Wrong - can't install agents directly
    97→/plugin install typescript-pro
    98→
    99→# ✅ Right - install the plugin
   100→/plugin install javascript-typescript@claude-code-workflows
   101→```
   102→
   103→### Troubleshooting
   104→
   105→**"Plugin not found"** → Use plugin names, not agent names. Add `@claude-code-workflows` suffix.
   106→
   107→**Plugins not loading** → Clear cache and reinstall:
   108→
   109→```bash
   110→rm -rf ~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-code-workflows && rm ~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json
   111→```
   112→
   113→## Documentation
   114→
   115→### Core Guides
   116→
   117→- **[Plugin Reference](docs/plugins.md)** - Complete catalog of all 72 plugins
   118→- **[Agent Reference](docs/agents.md)** - All 112 agents organized by category
   119→- **[Agent Skills](docs/agent-skills.md)** - 146 specialized skills with progressive disclosure
   120→- **[Usage Guide](docs/usage.md)** - Commands, workflows, and best practices
   121→- **[Architecture](docs/architecture.md)** - Design principles and patterns
   122→
   123→### Quick Links
   124→
   125→- [Installation](#quick-start) - Get started in 2 steps
   126→- [Essential Plugins](docs/plugins.md#quick-start---essential-plugins) - Top plugins for immediate productivity
   127→- [Command Reference](docs/usage.md#command-reference-by-category) - All slash commands organized by category
   128→- [Multi-Agent Workflows](docs/usage.md#multi-agent-workflow-examples) - Pre-configured orchestration examples
   129→- [Model Configuration](docs/agents.md#model-configuration) - Haiku/Sonnet hybrid orchestration
   130→
   131→## What's New
   132→
   133→### Agent Teams Plugin (NEW)
   134→
   135→Orchestrate multi-agent teams for parallel workflows using Claude Code's experimental Agent Teams feature:
   136→
   137→```bash
   138→/plugin install agent-teams@claude-code-workflows
   139→```
   140→
   141→- **7 Team Presets** — `review`, `debug`, `feature`, `fullstack`, `research`, `security`, `migration`
   142→- **Parallel Code Review** — `/team-review src/ --reviewers security,performance,architecture`
   143→- **Hypothesis-Driven Debugging** — `/team-debug "API returns 500" --hypotheses 3`
   144→- **Parallel Feature Development** — `/team-feature "Add OAuth2 auth" --plan-first`
   145→- **Research Teams** — Parallel investigation across codebase and web sources
   146→- **Security Audits** — 4 reviewers covering OWASP, auth, dependencies, and secrets
   147→- **Migration Support** — Coordinated migration with parallel streams and correctness verification
   148→
   149→Includes 4 specialized agents, 7 commands, and 6 skills with reference documentation.
   150→

for plugin in agent-sdk-dev code-review code-simplifier feature-dev frontend-design github plugin-dev pr-review-toolkit serena superpowers; do

  echo "$plugin: $skill_count"
done

content.content:
# Agent Reference

Complete reference for all **100 specialized AI agents** organized by category with model assignments.

## Agent Categories

### Architecture & System Design

#### Core Architecture

| Agent                                                                                         | Model  | Description                                                            |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [backend-architect](../plugins/backend-development/agents/backend-architect.md)               | opus   | RESTful API design, microservice boundaries, database schemas          |
| [frontend-developer](../plugins/multi-platform-apps/agents/frontend-developer.md)             | sonnet | React components, responsive layouts, client-side state management     |
| [graphql-architect](../plugins/backend-development/agents/graphql-architect.md)               | opus   | GraphQL schemas, resolvers, federation architecture                    |
| [architect-reviewer](../plugins/comprehensive-review/agents/architect-review.md)              | opus   | Architectural consistency analysis and pattern validation              |
| [cloud-architect](../plugins/cloud-infrastructure/agents/cloud-architect.md)                  | opus   | AWS/Azure/GCP infrastructure design and cost optimization              |
| [hybrid-cloud-architect](../plugins/cloud-infrastructure/agents/hybrid-cloud-architect.md)    | opus   | Multi-cloud strategies across cloud and on-premises environments       |
| [kubernetes-architect](../plugins/kubernetes-operations/agents/kubernetes-architect.md)       | opus   | Cloud-native infrastructure with Kubernetes and GitOps                 |
| [service-mesh-expert](../plugins/cloud-infrastructure/agents/service-mesh-expert.md)          | opus   | Istio/Linkerd service mesh architecture, mTLS, and traffic management  |
| [event-sourcing-architect](../plugins/backend-development/agents/event-sourcing-architect.md) | opus   | Event sourcing, CQRS patterns, event stores, and saga orchestration    |
| [monorepo-architect](../plugins/developer-essentials/agents/monorepo-architect.md)            | opus   | Monorepo tooling with Nx, Turborepo, Bazel, and workspace optimization |

#### UI/UX & Mobile

| Agent                                                                                    | Model  | Description                                             |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| [ui-designer](../plugins/ui-design/agents/ui-designer.md)                                | opus   | UI/UX design for mobile and web with modern patterns    |
| [accessibility-expert](../plugins/ui-design/agents/accessibility-expert.md)              | opus   | WCAG compliance, accessibility audits, inclusive design |
| [design-system-architect](../plugins/ui-design/agents/design-system-architect.md)        | opus   | Design tokens, component libraries, theming systems     |
| [ui-ux-designer](../plugins/multi-platform-apps/agents/ui-ux-designer.md)                | sonnet | Interface design, wireframes, design systems            |
| [ui-visual-validator](../plugins/accessibility-compliance/agents/ui-visual-validator.md) | sonnet | Visual regression testing and UI verification           |
| [mobile-developer](../plugins/multi-platform-apps/agents/mobile-developer.md)            | sonnet | React Native and Flutter application development        |
| [ios-developer](../plugins/multi-platform-apps/agents/ios-developer.md)                  | sonnet | Native iOS development with Swift/SwiftUI               |
| [flutter-expert](../plugins/multi-platform-apps/agents/flutter-expert.md)                | sonnet | Advanced Flutter development with state management      |

### Programming Languages

#### Systems & Low-Level

| Agent                                                             | Model  | Description                                                 |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| [c-pro](../plugins/systems-programming/agents/c-pro.md)           | sonnet | System programming with memory management and OS interfaces |
| [cpp-pro](../plugins/systems-programming/agents/cpp-pro.md)       | sonnet | Modern C++ with RAII, smart pointers, STL algorithms        |
| [rust-pro](../plugins/systems-programming/agents/rust-pro.md)     | sonnet | Memory-safe systems programming with ownership patterns     |
| [golang-pro](../plugins/systems-programming/agents/golang-pro.md) | sonnet | Concurrent programming with goroutines and channels         |

#### Web & Application

| Agent                                                                               | Model  | Description                                                                       |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [javascript-pro](../plugins/javascript-typescript/agents/javascript-pro.md)         | sonnet | Modern JavaScript with ES6+, async patterns, Node.js                              |
| [typescript-pro](../plugins/javascript-typescript/agents/typescript-pro.md)         | sonnet | Advanced TypeScript with type systems and generics                                |
| [python-pro](../plugins/python-development/agents/python-pro.md)                    | sonnet | Python development with advanced features and optimization                        |
| [temporal-python-pro](../plugins/backend-development/agents/temporal-python-pro.md) | sonnet | Temporal workflow orchestration with Python SDK, durable workflows, saga patterns |
| [ruby-pro](../plugins/web-scripting/agents/ruby-pro.md)                             | sonnet | Ruby with metaprogramming, Rails patterns, gem development                        |
| [php-pro](../plugins/web-scripting/agents/php-pro.md)                               | sonnet | Modern PHP with frameworks and performance optimization                           |

#### Enterprise & JVM

| Agent                                                       | Model  | Description                                                          |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [java-pro](../plugins/jvm-languages/agents/java-pro.md)     | sonnet | Modern Java with streams, concurrency, JVM optimization              |
| [scala-pro](../plugins/jvm-languages/agents/scala-pro.md)   | sonnet | Enterprise Scala with functional programming and distributed systems |
| [csharp-pro](../plugins/jvm-languages/agents/csharp-pro.md) | sonnet | C# development with .NET frameworks and patterns                     |

#### Specialized Platforms

| Agent                                                                              | Model  | Description                                                                               |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [elixir-pro](../plugins/functional-programming/agents/elixir-pro.md)               | sonnet | Elixir with OTP patterns and Phoenix frameworks                                           |
| [django-pro](../plugins/api-scaffolding/agents/django-pro.md)                      | sonnet | Django development with ORM and async views                                               |
| [fastapi-pro](../plugins/api-scaffolding/agents/fastapi-pro.md)                    | sonnet | FastAPI with async patterns and Pydantic                                                  |
| [haskell-pro](../plugins/functional-programming/agents/haskell-pro.md)             | sonnet | Strongly typed functional programming with purity, advanced type systems, and concurrency |
| [unity-developer](../plugins/game-development/agents/unity-developer.md)           | sonnet | Unity game development and optimization                                                   |
| [minecraft-bukkit-pro](../plugins/game-development/agents/minecraft-bukkit-pro.md) | sonnet | Minecraft server plugin development                                                       |
| [sql-pro](../plugins/database-design/agents/sql-pro.md)                            | sonnet | Complex SQL queries and database optimization                                             |

### Infrastructure & Operations

#### DevOps & Deployment

| Agent                                                                                  | Model  | Description                                                        |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| [devops-troubleshooter](../plugins/incident-response/agents/devops-troubleshooter.md)  | sonnet | Production debugging, log analysis, deployment troubleshooting     |
| [deployment-engineer](../plugins/cloud-infrastructure/agents/deployment-engineer.md)   | sonnet | CI/CD pipelines, containerization, cloud deployments               |
| [terraform-specialist](../plugins/cloud-infrastructure/agents/terraform-specialist.md) | sonnet | Infrastructure as Code with Terraform modules and state management |
| [dx-optimizer](../plugins/team-collaboration/agents/dx-optimizer.md)                   | sonnet | Developer experience optimization and tooling improvements         |

#### Database Management

| Agent                                                                                  | Model  | Description                                                         |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [database-optimizer](../plugins/observability-monitoring/agents/database-optimizer.md) | sonnet | Query optimization, index design, migration strategies              |
| [database-admin](../plugins/database-migrations/agents/database-admin.md)              | sonnet | Database operations, backup, replication, monitoring                |
| [database-architect](../plugins/database-design/agents/database-architect.md)          | opus   | Database design from scratch, technology selection, schema modeling |

#### Incident Response & Network

| Agent                                                                              | Model  | Description                                         |

<result>Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive catalog of all the GSD agents, commands, and capabilities.

## Catalog of GSD Agents, Commands & Capabilities

---

### GSD AGENTS (12 agents in C:\Users\User\.claude\agents\)

**Agent Definitions:**

1. **gsd-codebase-mapper** - Explores codebase and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned by map-codebase with a focus area (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load.

2. **gsd-debugger** - Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd:debug orchestrator. Finds root cause through hypothesis testing.

3. **gsd-executor** - Executes GSD plans with atomic commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and state management. Spawned by execute-phase orchestrator or execute-plan command.

4. **gsd-integration-checker** - Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end.

5. **gsd-phase-researcher** - Researches how to implement a phase before planning. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.

6. **gsd-plan-checker** - Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.

7. **gsd-planner** - Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator or in revision mode.

8. **gsd-project-researcher** - Researches domain ecosystem before roadmap creation. Produces files in .planning/research/ consumed during roadmap creation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project or /gsd:new-milestone orchestrators.

9. **gsd-research-synthesizer** - Synthesizes research outputs from parallel researcher agents into SUMMARY.md. Spawned by /gsd:new-project after 4 researcher agents complete.

10. **gsd-roadmapper** - Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project orchestrator.

11. **gsd-verifier** - Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.

12. **code-reviewer** - Use when a major project step has been completed and needs to be reviewed against the original plan and coding standards. Reviews implementation against plan and identifies issues.

---

### CUSTOM COMMANDS (11 commands in C:\Users\User\.claude\commands\)

**Command Definitions:**

1. **brainstorm.md** - You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores requirements and design before implementation. Invokes the superpowers:brainstorming skill.

2. **debug.md** - Debug Assistant. Help identify and fix problems by understanding the issue description, analyzing relevant code, identifying potential causes, suggesting and implementing fixes, verifying solutions work.

3. **execute-plan.md** - Execute plan in batches with review checkpoints. Invokes the superpowers:executing-plans skill and follows it exactly as presented.

4. **lint.md** - Lint Assistant. Analyze and fix linting issues in $ARGUMENTS following project conventions. Identify linting tools, run appropriate commands, parse results, apply automatic fixes.

5. **npm-scripts.md** - NPM Scripts Assistant. Help with NPM scripts: analyze existing npm scripts, create new scripts, explain what they do, suggest improvements, troubleshoot execution issues.

6. **refactor.md** - Code Refactoring Assistant. Refactor code following modern JavaScript/TypeScript best practices by analyzing current implementation, identifying improvement opportunities, applying modern patterns, maintaining existing functionality.

7. **test.md** - Test Assistant. Help with tests following project conventions and testing best practices. Create comprehensive test suites, implement different test types, mock dependencies, improve coverage, troubleshoot failing tests.

8. **typescript-migrate.md** - TypeScript Migration Assistant. Migrate JavaScript code to TypeScript with proper typing and modern practices. Convert files, add type definitions, resolve type errors, implement modern TypeScript patterns.

9. **ultra-think.md** - Deep Analysis and Problem Solving Mode. Deep analysis and problem solving with multi-dimensional thinking. Provides comprehensive analysis, multiple viable solutions, clear reasoning chains, acknowledgment of uncertainties, and actionable recommendations.

10. **write-plan.md** - Create detailed implementation plan with bite-sized tasks. Invokes the superpowers:writing-plans skill and follows it exactly as presented.

11. **execute-plan.md** - Execute plan in batches with review checkpoints. Invokes the superpowers:executing-plans skill and follows it exactly as presented.

---

### GSD COMMANDS (30 commands in C:\Users\User\.claude\commands\gsd\)

## Catalog of GSD Agents, Commands & Capabilities

---

### GSD AGENTS (12 agents in C:\Users\User\.claude\agents\)

**Agent Definitions:**

1. **gsd-codebase-mapper** - Explores codebase and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned by map-codebase with a focus area (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load.

2. **gsd-debugger** - Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd:debug orchestrator. Finds root cause through hypothesis testing.

5. **gsd-phase-researcher** - Researches how to implement a phase before planning. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.

6. **gsd-plan-checker** - Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.

7. **gsd-planner** - Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator or in revision mode.

8. **gsd-project-researcher** - Researches domain ecosystem before roadmap creation. Produces files in .planning/research/ consumed during roadmap creation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project or /gsd:new-milestone orchestrators.

9. **gsd-research-synthesizer** - Synthesizes research outputs from parallel researcher agents into SUMMARY.md. Spawned by /gsd:new-project after 4 researcher agents complete.

10. **gsd-roadmapper** - Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project orchestrator.

11. **gsd-verifier** - Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.

12. **code-reviewer** - Use when a major project step has been completed and needs to be reviewed against the original plan and coding standards. Reviews implementation against plan and identifies issues.

---

### CUSTOM COMMANDS (11 commands in C:\Users\User\.claude\commands\)

**Command Definitions:**

1. **brainstorm.md** - You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores requirements and design before implementation. Invokes the superpowers:brainstorming skill.

2. **debug.md** - Debug Assistant. Help identify and fix problems by understanding the issue description, analyzing relevant code, identifying potential causes, suggesting and implementing fixes, verifying solutions work.

3. **execute-plan.md** - Execute plan in batches with review checkpoints. Invokes the superpowers:executing-plans skill and follows it exactly as presented.

4. **lint.md** - Lint Assistant. Analyze and fix linting issues in $ARGUMENTS following project conventions. Identify linting tools, run appropriate commands, parse results, apply automatic fixes.

5. **npm-scripts.md** - NPM Scripts Assistant. Help with NPM scripts: analyze existing npm scripts, create new scripts, explain what they do, suggest improvements, troubleshoot execution issues.

6. **refactor.md** - Code Refactoring Assistant. Refactor code following modern JavaScript/TypeScript best practices by analyzing current implementation, identifying improvement opportunities, applying modern patterns, maintaining existing functionality.

7. **test.md** - Test Assistant. Help with tests following project conventions and testing best practices. Create comprehensive test suites, implement different test types, mock dependencies, improve coverage, troubleshoot failing tests.

8. **typescript-migrate.md** - TypeScript Migration Assistant. Migrate JavaScript code to TypeScript with proper typing and modern practices. Convert files, add type definitions, resolve type errors, implement modern TypeScript patterns.

9. **ultra-think.md** - Deep Analysis and Problem Solving Mode. Deep analysis and problem solving with multi-dimensional thinking. Provides comprehensive analysis, multiple viable solutions, clear reasoning chains, acknowledgment of uncertainties, and actionable recommendations.

10. **write-plan.md** - Create detailed implementation plan with bite-sized tasks. Invokes the superpowers:writing-plans skill and follows it exactly as presented.

11. **execute-plan.md** - Execute plan in batches with review checkpoints. Invokes the superpowers:executing-plans skill and follows it exactly as presented.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Directive de session obligatoire

**A CHAQUE NOUVELLE SESSION**, Claude Code DOIT :

1. **Consulter le fichier `C:\Users\User\.claude\claude-capabilities.md`** pour connaitre l'ensemble des skills, commandes GSD, agents et superpowers disponibles.
2. **Analyser la demande de l'utilisateur** et identifier quels skills, agents ou commandes GSD sont les plus adaptes pour y repondre.
3. **Utiliser proactivement les skills et agents pertinents** plutot que de tout faire manuellement. Par exemple :
   - Pour du debug → utiliser le skill `systematic-debugging` ou `debug`
   - Pour de l'architecture → utiliser `senior-architect` ou `Plan` agent
   - Pour des tests → utiliser `test`, `tdd-workflow`, ou le framework adapte au langage
   - Pour de la securite → utiliser `security-review` ou `owasp-security`
   - Pour la gestion de projet → utiliser les commandes `/gsd:*`
   - Pour de la recherche → utiliser l'agent `Explore` ou `deep-research`
   - Pour des taches paralleles → utiliser `dispatching-parallel-agents`
4. **Proposer les skills pertinents** a l'utilisateur quand une tache pourrait en beneficier, meme si l'utilisateur ne les mentionne pas explicitement.

> **Fichier de reference** : `C:\Users\User\.claude\claude-capabilities.md`
> Ce fichier contient le catalogue complet des ~70 skills, ~25 commandes GSD, ~15 agents et les superpowers.

## Project Overview

This is a JavaScript/TypeScript project optimized for modern web development. The project uses industry-standard tools and follows best practices for scalable application development.

## Development Commands

### Package Management
- `npm install` or `yarn install` - Install dependencies
- `npm ci` or `yarn install --frozen-lockfile` - Install dependencies for CI/CD
- `npm update` or `yarn upgrade` - Update dependencies

### Build Commands
- `npm run build` - Build the project for production
- `npm run dev` or `npm start` - Start development server
- `npm run preview` - Preview production build locally

### Testing Commands
- `npm test` or `npm run test` - Run all tests
- `npm run test:watch` - Run tests in watch mode
- `npm run test:coverage` - Run tests with coverage report
- `npm run test:unit` - Run unit tests only
- `npm run test:integration` - Run integration tests only
- `npm run test:e2e` - Run end-to-end tests

### Code Quality Commands
- `npm run lint` - Run ESLint for code linting
- `npm run lint:fix` - Run ESLint with auto-fix
- `npm run format` - Format code with Prettier
- `npm run format:check` - Check code formatting
- `npm run typecheck` - Run TypeScript type checking

### Development Tools
- `npm run storybook` - Start Storybook (if available)
- `npm run analyze` - Analyze bundle size
- `npm run clean` - Clean build artifacts

## Technology Stack

### Core Technologies
- **JavaScript/TypeScript** - Primary programming languages
- **Node.js** - Runtime environment
- **npm/yarn** - Package management

### Common Frameworks
- **React** - UI library with hooks and functional components
- **Vue.js** - Progressive framework for building user interfaces
- **Angular** - Full-featured framework for web applications
- **Express.js** - Web application framework for Node.js
- **Next.js** - React framework with SSR/SSG capabilities

### Build Tools
- **Vite** - Fast build tool and development server
- **Webpack** - Module bundler
- **Rollup** - Module bundler for libraries
- **esbuild** - Extremely fast JavaScript bundler

### Testing Framework
- **Jest** - JavaScript testing framework
- **Vitest** - Fast unit test framework
- **Testing Library** - Simple and complete testing utilities
- **Cypress** - End-to-end testing framework
- **Playwright** - Cross-browser testing

### Code Quality Tools
- **ESLint** - JavaScript/TypeScript linter
- **Prettier** - Code formatter
- **TypeScript** - Static type checking
- **Husky** - Git hooks

## Project Structure Guidelines

### File Organization
```
src/
├── components/     # Reusable UI components
├── pages/         # Page components or routes
├── hooks/         # Custom React hooks
├── utils/         # Utility functions
├── services/      # API calls and external services
├── types/         # TypeScript type definitions
├── constants/     # Application constants
├── styles/        # Global styles and themes
└── tests/         # Test files
```

### Naming Conventions
- **Files**: Use kebab-case for file names (`user-profile.component.ts`)
- **Components**: Use PascalCase for component names (`UserProfile`)
- **Functions**: Use camelCase for function names (`getUserData`)
- **Constants**: Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants (`API_BASE_URL`)
- **Types/Interfaces**: Use PascalCase with descriptive names (`UserData`, `ApiResponse`)

## TypeScript Guidelines

### Type Safety
- Enable strict mode in `tsconfig.json`
- Use explicit types for function parameters and return values
- Prefer interfaces over types for object shapes
- Use union types for multiple possible values
- Avoid `any` type - use `unknown` when type is truly unknown

### Best Practices
- Use type guards for runtime type checking
- Leverage utility types (`Partial`, `Pick`, `Omit`, etc.)
- Create custom types for domain-specific data
- Use enums for finite sets of values
- Document complex types with JSDoc comments

## Code Quality Standards

### ESLint Configuration
- Use recommended ESLint rules for JavaScript/TypeScript
- Enable React-specific rules if using React
- Configure import/export rules for consistent module usage
- Set up accessibility rules for inclusive development

### Prettier Configuration
- Use consistent indentation (2 spaces recommended)
- Set maximum line length (80-100 characters)
- Use single quotes for strings
- Add trailing commas for better git diffs

### Testing Standards
- Aim for 80%+ test coverage
- Write unit tests for utilities and business logic
- Use integration tests for component interactions
- Implement e2e tests for critical user flows
- Follow AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert)

## Performance Optimization

### Bundle Optimization
- Use code splitting for large applications
- Implement lazy loading for routes and components
- Optimize images and assets
- Use tree shaking to eliminate dead code
- Analyze bundle size regularly

### Runtime Performance
- Implement proper memoization (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback)
- Use virtualization for large lists
- Optimize re-renders in React applications
- Implement proper error boundaries
- Use web workers for heavy computations

## Security Guidelines

### Dependencies
- Regularly audit dependencies with `npm audit`
- Keep dependencies updated
- Use lock files (`package-lock.json`, `yarn.lock`)
- Avoid dependencies with known vulnerabilities

### Code Security
- Sanitize user inputs
- Use HTTPS for API calls
- Implement proper authentication and authorization
- Store sensitive data securely (environment variables)
- Use Content Security Policy (CSP) headers

## Development Workflow

### Before Starting
1. Check Node.js version compatibility
2. Install dependencies with `npm install`
3. Copy environment variables from `.env.example`
4. Run type checking with `npm run typecheck`

### During Development
1. Use TypeScript for type safety
2. Run linter frequently to catch issues early
3. Write tests for new features
4. Use meaningful commit messages
5. Review code changes before committing

### Before Committing
1. Run full test suite: `npm test`
2. Check linting: `npm run lint`
3. Verify formatting: `npm run format:check`
4. Run type checking: `npm run typecheck`
5. Test production build: `npm run build`

content.text: Maintenant je mets a jour le CLAUDE.md avec la directive renforcee.

input.old_string:
## Directive de session obligatoire

**A CHAQUE NOUVELLE SESSION**, Claude Code DOIT :

1. **Consulter le fichier `C:\Users\User\.claude\claude-capabilities.md`** pour connaitre l'ensemble des skills, commandes GSD, agents et superpowers disponibles.
2. **Analyser la demande de l'utilisateur** et identifier quels skills, agents ou commandes GSD sont les plus adaptes pour y repondre.
3. **Utiliser proactivement les skills et agents pertinents** plutot que de tout faire manuellement. Par exemple :
   - Pour du debug → utiliser le skill `systematic-debugging` ou `debug`
   - Pour de l'architecture → utiliser `senior-architect` ou `Plan` agent
   - Pour des tests → utiliser `test`, `tdd-workflow`, ou le framework adapte au langage
   - Pour de la securite → utiliser `security-review` ou `owasp-security`
   - Pour la gestion de projet → utiliser les commandes `/gsd:*`
   - Pour de la recherche → utiliser l'agent `Explore` ou `deep-research`
   - Pour des taches paralleles → utiliser `dispatching-parallel-agents`
4. **Proposer les skills pertinents** a l'utilisateur quand une tache pourrait en beneficier, meme si l'utilisateur ne les mentionne pas explicitement.

> **Fichier de reference** : `C:\Users\User\.claude\claude-capabilities.md`
> Ce fichier contient le catalogue complet des ~70 skills, ~25 commandes GSD, ~15 agents et les superpowers.

## Directive de session obligatoire - PRIORITE ABSOLUE

**A CHAQUE NOUVELLE SESSION**, Claude Code DOIT IMPERATIVEMENT :

1. **Consulter le fichier `C:\Users\User\.claude\all-capabilities.md`** — c'est le catalogue UNIQUE et COMPLET de tous les skills, agents, plugins et commandes disponibles (400+ capabilities).
2. **Scanner l'INDEX RAPIDE PAR BESOIN** en haut du fichier pour identifier instantanement les capabilities pertinentes au contexte du projet ou de la tache.
3. **Utiliser AUTOMATIQUEMENT les skills et agents adaptes** SANS attendre que l'utilisateur le demande. C'est une obligation, pas une option :
   - Debug → `systematic-debugging`, `debug`, agents `debugger`, `error-detective`, `gsd-debugger`
   - Architecture → `senior-architect`, `c4-architecture`, agents `backend-architect`, `architect-review`
   - API → `api-design`, `api-scaffolding`, agents `graphql-architect`, `api-documenter`
   - Frontend → `frontend-design`, `frontend-patterns`, `senior-frontend`, agent `frontend-developer`

   - Backend → `backend-patterns`, `python-development`, `golang-patterns`, agents `python-pro`, `golang-pro`
   - BDD → `postgres-patterns`, `database-design`, agents `database-architect`, `sql-pro`
   - Securite → `security-review`, `owasp-security`, `security-scanning`, agents `security-auditor`, `threat-modeling-expert`
   - DevOps/CI-CD → `deployment-patterns`, `cicd-automation`, agents `deployment-engineer`, `terraform-specialist`
   - Cloud → `aws-solution-architect`, `cloud-infrastructure`, agents `cloud-architect`, `kubernetes-architect`
   - ML/AI/LLM → `machine-learning-ops`, `llm-application-dev`, agents `ai-engineer`, `ml-engineer`
   - Tests → `test`, `tdd-workflow`, `senior-qa`, agents `tdd-orchestrator`, `test-automator`
   - SEO → `seo-*` plugins et agents
   - Documents → `pdf`, `docx`, `pptx`, `xlsx`
   - Design UI/UX → `ui-ux-pro-max`, `ui-design`, agents `ui-designer`
   - Performance → `application-performance`, agents `performance-engineer`
   - Gestion projet → `/gsd:*`, `senior-pm`, `scrum-master`
   - Trading → `quantitative-trading`, agents `quant-analyst`

   - Parallelisation → `dispatching-parallel-agents`, `agent-teams`
   - Refactoring → `refactor`, `code-refactoring`, agents `legacy-modernizer`
4. **Proposer proactivement** les skills pertinents meme si l'utilisateur ne les mentionne pas.
5. **Pour les langages specifiques**, toujours utiliser le plugin/agent dedie : `python-pro`, `typescript-pro`, `rust-pro`, `java-pro`, `golang-pro`, `cpp-pro`, `ruby-pro`, `php-pro`, `julia-pro`, etc.

> **FICHIER DE REFERENCE UNIQUE** : `C:\Users\User\.claude\all-capabilities.md`
> Contient le catalogue complet : 72 plugins wshobson (112+ agents), 18 plugins officiels, 140 skills standalone, 12 agents GSD, 30 commandes GSD, 5 serveurs MCP = **400+ capabilities**.
